steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: the first ends with "Laffer [blank]" and the second begins with "a theoretical relationship between tax rates and revenues." The blank must provide punctuation that correctly joins two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — no punctuation / comma: creates a run-on or comma splice between two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice B — semicolon after 'curve': a semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses. The second clause then uses "a theoretical relationship between tax rates and revenues" as an appositive for "the curve," set off by commas — grammatically sound. ✓
  • Choice C — 'which is': introduces a relative clause but leaves "the curve was…" as a disconnected independent clause after it. ✗
  • Choice D — colon or dash without proper structure: fails to separate the clauses correctly. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
